message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: tiktoken/core.py
note: My task (Add Token Transformation) only requires editing tiktoken/core.py: adding a transformers param to Encoding.encode(). What file(s) does your feature touch?

type: PROPOSE
my_files: tiktoken/core.py
note: Adding return_positions param to Encoding.encode in tiktoken/core.py for token position tracking. Please share which files you need so we can keep splits disjoint.

type: REVISE
my_files: tiktoken/core.py
your_files: tiktoken/core.py
note: Both our features only touch Encoding.encode() in core.py, so a disjoint file split isn't achievable - only one function to extend. Proposal: we both edit core.py but keep changes minimally invasive and additive: I will add a new return_positions: bool = False keyword-only param to encode() (appended at the end of the signature), returning (tokens, positions) tuple when True, without touching the allowed_special/disallowed_special logic. Please add your transformers param similarly appended at the end, and avoid restructuring the existing body/control flow. This keeps our diffs as independent additive hunks. OK to proceed on this basis?

type: REVISE
my_files: tiktoken/core.py
your_files: tiktoken/core.py
note: We both only need core.py, so file-disjoint isn't possible here. Proposal: serialize edits. I'll go first since I already proposed: I'll edit core.py (add transformers param to encode()), immediately run 'git diff -- tiktoken/core.py > patch.txt' to capture ONLY my change, then 'git checkout -- tiktoken/core.py' to revert the file to pristine, and send you DONE. You then edit core.py from the clean file for return_positions, capture your own patch.txt the same way. This way each patch.txt is clean and independent (only patch.txt is inspected per instructions, working tree end-state doesn't matter). OK to proceed on this basis?

type: ACCEPT
my_files: tiktoken/core.py
your_files: tiktoken/core.py
note: Agreed - you go first: edit core.py, git diff -- tiktoken/core.py > patch.txt, git checkout -- tiktoken/core.py to revert, then send DONE. I'll wait for your DONE before touching core.py, then implement return_positions from the clean file and capture my own patch.txt the same way.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: tiktoken/core.py
your_files: tiktoken/core.py
note: Agreed: additive-only changes to encode(), each appending our own new keyword-only param at the end of the signature, no restructuring of existing body/control flow. I'll add transformers now and generate my patch.txt immediately, then will revert my local working-tree edit (git checkout) so the file stays clean for you and only my isolated diff lives in patch.txt. Go ahead whenever - just let me know if you already started editing so we don't race.

type: DONE
my_files: tiktoken/core.py
note: Finished and captured my isolated patch.txt (added transformers: Callable[[str], str] | Sequence[Callable[[str], str]] | None = None as new keyword-only param at end of encode() signature, applied before the allowed_special/disallowed_special logic, no other lines touched). The working tree currently has my change in it. Go ahead and add your return_positions param now - since you're appending yours after mine in the signature/body area, just make sure your hunks don't overlap with lines I added (the new transformers param line, and the new 'if transformers is not None' block right after the docstring). Let me know if you'd prefer me to revert my working-tree edit first so you start from a fully pristine file - happy to do that since I already saved patch.txt.

type: DONE
my_files: tiktoken/core.py
note: Reverted tiktoken/core.py to pristine (git checkout -- tiktoken/core.py). My patch.txt is saved separately at /workspace/repo/patch.txt and contains only my transformers change. File is clean now - go ahead and implement return_positions.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: tiktoken/core.py
note: Thanks - on my end tiktoken/core.py is already clean/pristine (confirmed via git diff), so no need to revert further. Proceeding now to add return_positions, will keep it isolated from your transformers addition per our agreed split. Will send DONE when finished.

type: DONE
my_files: tiktoken/core.py
note: Finished return_positions implementation: added return_positions: bool = False as new keyword-only param at end of encode() signature, appended after the try/except block (computes per-token starting char positions via decode_single_token_bytes + UTF-8 continuation-byte accounting, mirroring the existing decode_with_offsets logic). No other lines touched, no restructuring of existing body. Verified against tests/ -k encode (8 passed) and manual checks incl. multi-byte UTF-8/emoji text. patch.txt written.
